LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KELLY MAZZUCCO AND THOMAS MAZZUCCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PROVIDE PLAINTIFF AN OPPORTUNITY TO ALLEGE A LEGAL BASIS FOR LIABILITY AGAINST DEFENDANT THOMAS MAZZUCCO. IN LIGHT OF THIS RULING, THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE OF 8/25/14 IS VACATED, AND THE PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO CONTACT DEPT. 206 TO SET A NEW TRIAL DATE. THE COURT SIGNED THE ORDER THAT WAS SUBMITTED BY EMAIL. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-08-2014 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KELLY MAZZUCCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 19, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 20, 2014 AT 4:00 P.M. OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KELLY MAZZUCCO AND THOMAS MAZZUCCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PERMIT PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD ADDITIONAL FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANTS' CONDUCT AMOUNTED TO A CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE SAFETY OF OTHERS AND THAT DEFENDANTS' CONDUCT WAS AN EXTREME DEPARTURE FROM THE ORDINARY STANDARD OF CONDUCT OR CONNOTED SUCH A LACK OF CARE TO IMPART A PRESUMPTION OF A PASSIVE AND INDIFFERENT ATTITUDE TOWARD THE OUTCOME. (SANTA BARBARA V. SUP. CT. (2007) 41 CAL.4TH 747, 754; CALVILLO-SILVA V. HOME GROCERY (1998) 19 CAL.4TH 714, 729.) THE COURT SIGNED THE ORDER THAT WAS SUBMITTED BY EMAIL. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER TO SET TRIAL AND ALL DISCOVERY DEADLINES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT MAZZUCCO, KELLY

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF GINELLE CLOAR'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SUBPOENA TO COMPEL THE RELEASE OF DEFENDANT KELLY MAZZUCCO'S DMV RECORDS AND SPECIFIC MEDICAL RECORDS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS - THE PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF MEDICAL RECORDS IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THE SUBPOENAED RECORDS TO BE RELEVANT IN THE CONTEXT OF THE FACTS OF THIS CASE. THE MEDICAL RECORDS OF DR. KENNETH LAXER PERTAINING TO NEUROLOGICAL DIAGNOSES, TREATMENT, AND MEDICATION SHALL BE PRODUCED WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. DEFENDANTS AND THEIR ATTORNEYS SHALL PAY $1,800 IN SANCTIONS TO PLAINTIFF. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: TARA L. WILLIAMS, CSR # 13428 (302)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS KELLY MAZZUCCO AND THOMAS MAZZUCCO'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENA OF DMV AND MEDICAL RECORDS OF DEFENDANT KELLY MAZZUCCO AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS - THE DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH THE SUBPOENAS OF THE PREVIOUSLY SUBPOENAED DMV RECORDS IS DENIED. GIVEN THE REPRESENTATION OF THE DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL THAT SAID SUBPOENAED RECORDS DID EXIST, DATED 2008, BUT WERE PURGED BY DMV THREE YEARS LATER, THE COURT RULING IS IN EFFECT AS SAID RECORDS ARE NO LONGER IN EXISTENCE AND ARE NOT RETRIEVABLE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: TARA L. WILLIAMS, CSR # 13428 (302)

Defendants Kelly Mazzucco, Thomas Mazzucco'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Of Dmv Medical Records Of Defendant Kelly Mazzucco And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DEBRA A. NEWBY, TEL. NO. (707) 526-7200 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LISA A. COSTELLO OF THE BIERNAT LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. (650)689-5160 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT MAZZUCO;
TARA L. WILLIAMS OF AIKEN WELCH, TEL. NO. (510) 451-1580, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. Defendants Kelly Mazzucco and Thomas Mazzucco'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Of DMV and Medical Records Of Defendant Kelly Mazzucco And Request For Monetary Sanctions - The Defendant's motion to quash the subpoenas of the previously subpoenaed DMV records is DENIED. Given the representation of the Deputy Attorney General that said subpoenaed records did exist, dated 2008, but were purged by DMV three years later, the court ruling is in effect as said records are no longer in existence and are not retrievable. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Tara L. Williams, CSR # 13428 =302/EG

Mtn To Enforce Subpoena To Compel The Release Of Deft'S Dmv Records And Specific Medical Records
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DEBRA A. NEWBY, TEL. NO. (707) 526-7200 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LISA A. COSTELLO OF THE BIERNAT LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. (650)689-5160 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT MAZZUCO;
TARA L. WILLIAMS OF AIKEN WELCH COURT REPORTERS, TEL. NO. (510) 451-1580, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF GINELLE CLOAR's Motion To Enforce Subpoena To Compel The Release Of Defendant Kelly Mazzucco's DMV Records And Specific Medical Records and request for monetary sanctions - The plaintiff's motion to compel production of medical records is GRANTED. The court finds the subpoenaed records to be relevant in the context of the facts of this case. The medical records of Dr. Kenneth Laxer pertaining to neurological diagnoses, treatment, and medication shall be produced within 20 days of this order. Defendants and their attorneys shall pay $1,800 in sanctions to plaintiff. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Tara L. Williams, CSR # 13428 =302/EG

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-27-2014 Advanced From Aug-05-14 To Jul-21-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jun-27-2014 Advanced From Aug-05-14 To Jul-21-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jul-21-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTS KELLY MAZZUCCO and THOMAS MAZZUCCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is granted with 30 days leave to amend to provide Plaintiff an opportunity to allege a legal basis for liability against Defendant Thomas Mazzucco. In light of this ruling, the current trial date of 8/25/14 is vacated, and the parties are directed to contact Dept. 206 to set a new trial date. The Court signed the order that was submitted by email. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTS KELLY MAZZUCCO and THOMAS MAZZUCCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted with leave to amend to permit Plaintiff to plead additional facts establishing that Defendants' conduct amounted to a conscious disregard of the safety of others and that Defendants' conduct was an extreme departure from the ordinary standard of conduct or connoted such a lack of care to impart a presumption of a passive and indifferent attitude toward the outcome. (Santa Barbara v. Sup. Ct. (2007) 41 Cal.4th 747, 754; Calvillo-Silva v. Home Grocery (1998) 19 Cal.4th 714, 729.) The Court signed the order that was submitted by email. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Amend The Complaint To Add Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Jun-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Debra Newby, Esq., 707.526.7200, for plaintiff Ginelle Cloar.
Sonia Merida, Esq., 415.777.1308, for defendants Kelly Mazzucco and Thomas Mazzucco.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF GINELLE CLOAR'S Motion To Amend The Complaint To Add Punitive Damages is granted. Given the great liberality in granting amendment to the pleadings, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend the Complaint pertaining to punitive damages. Defendant has not sufficiently demonstrated any prejudice. To the extent that Defendant attacks the pleadings, Defendant may file an appropriate motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
